dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the captain knows we've seen the ledgers. Things are moving fast.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Two guards loyal to the captain came to the tavern asking pointed questions about where I've been and who I've been talking to. {target_npc} was warned to stay quiet: a thinly veiled threat disguised as friendly advice. The captain is trying to move the evidence: I saw crates being loaded onto a cart heading {location_hint} under heavy escort. If that cart reaches its destination, the proof disappears forever. I need you to intercept it or at least follow it to wherever they're hiding the records. Watch out for {enemy_type} along the road: the captain might be using them as a screen.
dialogueChunks.outro: The captain is panicking. Panicked people are dangerous, but they also make mistakes. Don't let the evidence vanish.

playerResponses.text: A cart full of evidence heading out of town. I'll follow it.
Panicked and dangerous. My favorite combination. I'm on it.
Following an armed escort into the wilderness? That's how people disappear.

dialogueChunks.intro: Someone broke into my home last night, {player_name}. They took the proof I'd been keeping.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The vials, the private ledger, everything I'd documented about the diluted medicines: stolen. The healer must have realized I know. {target_npc} says the healer has been acting erratically, canceling appointments, avoiding people. They're planning to run. I need you to find where they hid the stolen evidence: {target_npc} saw them heading toward an old storage building {location_hint}. And I need you to gather {quest_item} urgently: the patients on diluted medicines need real treatment before anyone else gets worse.
dialogueChunks.outro: They stole back the proof, but they can't steal back what's in my memory. If we can recover even one piece of evidence, the council will listen.

playerResponses.text: The evidence is out there. I'll track it down and bring proper medicine too.
Breaking into your home to destroy proof. Desperation makes people bold.
If they're willing to break into homes, they're willing to do worse. Too dangerous.

dialogueChunks.intro: They did it, {player_name}. {target_npc} went to the council and accused me of the theft.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: False charges, fabricated ledgers, character witnesses who owe them favors: the whole thing was prepared in advance, like they knew this day was coming. I have three days before the council rules. If I can't produce the real records and the hidden cache of stolen goods, I'm the one who gets exiled. I need you to find that cache {location_hint}: the actual stolen goods with {target_npc}'s markings still on them. The {enemy_type} in the area will make it difficult, and I suspect {target_npc} may have set up obstacles of their own. This is my last chance.
dialogueChunks.outro: Three days to prove I'm not the thief. Three days to prove my hero is. Everything rides on what you find in that cache.

playerResponses.text: Three days. I won't waste a minute. Where's the cache?
Framing the accuser. Classic move. I'll find the real evidence.
If the council already has their mind made up, evidence won't help.
